text.title: BUG (defect) flow
text.summary: BUG has a richer file shape than CR (repro + root cause + regression test) and a three-state lifecycle because the repro step warrants visibility.

rule.statement: A BUG file MUST contain five sections: Symptom, Repro, Root cause, Fix, Regression test.
rule.rationale: Repro and root-cause are what distinguish a BUG from a CR — without them the fix is conjecture.

example.code:
# BUG019: signup form accepts whitespace-only password
linked-feature: F012
## Symptom User submits " " (3 spaces) as password → account created, login impossible.
## Repro 1. Visit /signup 2. Email: test@x.com, Password: " " 3. Submit → 201 Created 4. Try login with same creds → 401.
## Root cause `UserSerializer.validate_password` trims whitespace AFTER length check; the length check ran on the un-trimmed string.
## Fix Trim first, then length-check. Add `MIN_LEN_AFTER_TRIM = 8`.
## Regression test `tests/api/test_signup.py::test_password_whitespace_rejected`.

rule.rationale: Repro work is real work; the in-progress state makes it visible to anyone scanning the queue.

rule.statement: Commits implementing a BUG fix MUST use prefix `fix(BUG0NN): <short title>`.
rule.rationale: Symmetric with `cr(CR0NN): ...`. Allows machine-grouped release notes.

antipattern.description: Closing a BUG without writing a regression test.
antipattern.reason: The defect comes back. The five-section file shape exists precisely so the test step cannot be skipped.
